title: Backdate dates into timeline
excerpt:
Description
I’d love the ability to enter a specific launch date (using either a Timeline or standard Date column), pair it with a number in a Numbers column, and have monday.com automatically backdate and generate timel…

posts.author: monicaedie3 (Monica)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

I’d love the ability to enter a specific launch date (using either a Timeline or standard Date column), pair it with a number in a Numbers column, and have monday.com automatically backdate and generate timelines for all subitems based on that launch date. Essentially, the system would read the launch date, subtract the number of days specified in each subitem’s Numbers column, and then set each subitem’s timeline accordingly.

What are you trying to achieve

This would make recurring, repeatable timelines—especially for marketing campaigns—far more efficient and consistent.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
I actually really like this idea. Back-scheduling from a launch date is a very common way even product teams at my company work.

The one thing I keep wondering about is how monday would know what to anchor the schedule to.

For a feature like this to work, the system would need a way to determine things like:

  • what the final milestone or launch date is

  • which item should act as the anchor

  • whether the schedule should run forward from the first task or backward from the final milestone

So it might need something like:

  • a way to identify the final milestone

  • then some type of switch to schedule backward from that date once dependencies are in place

Otherwise the platform wouldn’t know whether it should build the schedule from the beginning of the project or from the end.

One other thought. If you have access to Sidekick, I wonder if something like this could potentially be generated there already. In theory you could describe the campaign structure, the launch date, and the offsets, and have Sidekick build or adjust the timelines for the subitems. It might not be exactly the same as a native feature, but it could possibly get close depending on how the board is structured.

Really interesting use case though. I could definitely see teams planning launches, campaigns, or events benefiting from something like this.
